findings.description: Footer text appears at the beginning of the reading order.
findings.evidence: The footer note from the PDF ('* Times indicated are merely for guidance...') is rendered at the very top of the HTML page 1, before the main title.

findings.description: Agenda schedule layout changed from aligned columns to a single-column bulleted list.
findings.evidence: The PDF aligns times in a left column and agenda items on the right. The HTML renders these as a single-column, nested bulleted list.

findings.description: Bulleted list items flattened into a single run-on paragraph.
findings.evidence: At the top of HTML Page 2, the sub-items for the 10:00 a.m. agenda entry (which appeared at the bottom of PDF Page 1) are rendered as a single continuous paragraph with asterisks acting as literal text, completely losing the list formatting.

findings.description: Header and footer text injected into the middle of the main content flow.
findings.evidence: On HTML Page 2, the document title and the footer note ('* Times indicated...') are inserted between the flattened 10:00 a.m. text block and the 'Informational Materials continued:' section.

findings.description: Page footer text from the second page is improperly injected into the content flow and duplicated twice within the HTML.
findings.evidence: The text 'Times indicated are merely for guidance. The commission will proceed through the agenda until completed. **Probable Action Items 2' appears twice in the latter half of the HTML: once immediately preceding 'Informational Materials continued:' and again at the very end of the list after '4:00 p.m. Adjourn'.

findings.description: Page headers and footers are interleaved directly into the main content lists, disrupting the structural flow of the agenda.
findings.evidence: The page 2 header 'Marine Fisheries Commission Business Meeting Agenda' and its corresponding footer break the <ul> list items between the 10:00 a.m. section and the 'Informational Materials continued' subsection, making the header/footer text render as list items rather than being omitted or isolated.
